name: Distributed MapReduce: HTTP Status Code Counter
description:
This architecture demonstrates the MapReduce paradigm used to process a 1GB log file across a distributed cluster. The goal is to aggregate the frequency of HTTP status codes (e.g., 200, 404, 500).

  • Input Splitting: The 1GB file is divided into 128MB chunks (InputSplits) so multiple workers can process data in parallel.
  • Map Phase: Each Mapper reads its assigned split line-by-line, parsing the status code and emitting a key-value pair: (status_code, 1).
  • Shuffle & Sort: This critical intermediate step groups all values associated with the same key. A Hash Partitioner determines which Reducer handles which key (e.g., Hash(200) % NumReducers).
  • Reduce Phase: Each Reducer receives a key and a list of values (200, [1, 1, 1...]), sums them up, and writes the final count to HDFS.
code:
graph LR
  subgraph Input_Layer [Data Distribution]
    File[1GB Access Log] --> S1[Split 1: 128MB]
    File --> S2[Split 2: 128MB]
    File --> Sn[Split N: 128MB]
  end

  subgraph Map_Phase [Parallel Mapping]
    S1 --> M1[Mapper 1]
    S2 --> M2[Mapper 2]
    Sn --> Mn[Mapper N]
    M1 -- "Emit" --> KV1["200, 1"]
    M2 -- "Emit" --> KV2["404, 1"]
    Mn -- "Emit" --> KVn["200, 1"]
  end

  subgraph Shuffle_Mechanism [Partitioning & Sorting]
    KV1 --> HP[Hash Partitioner]
    KV2 --> HP
    KVn --> HP
    HP -- "Key: 200" --> R1
    HP -- "Key: 404" --> R2
    HP -- "Key: 500" --> R3
    note["Logic: Hash(Key) % ReducerCount"] -.-> HP
  end

  subgraph Reduce_Phase [Aggregation]
    R1[Reducer 1] -- "Summing" --> C1[Count 200]
    R2[Reducer 2] -- "Summing" --> C2[Count 404]
    R3[Reducer 3] -- "Summing" --> C3[Count 500]
  end

  subgraph Storage [Output]
    C1 --> Out[(HDFS: Final Result)]
    C2 --> Out
    C3 --> Out
  end

  style HP fill:#f9f,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px
  style File fill:#fff,stroke:#333
  style Out fill:#dfd,stroke:#333
